name: securitycontext
description: Security Context (ProjectDiscovery): mines a PUBLIC GitHub repo's fix-commit history + disclosed CVEs into a hunting brief, recurring weak spots, and ranked variant leads — and also searches ProjectDiscovery's live vulnerability DB (CVEs/KEV/Nuclei templates). No key, public repos only; results are public, served best-effort. FOUR read tools (prefer these; unmetered): get_security_context + get_vulnerability_leads take a repo (owner/name or github URL); search_vulnerabilities takes a Bleve-syntax query (e.g. 'severity:critical && is_kev:true', 'affected_products.product:tomcat', 'is_poc:true'); get_vulnerability takes an id (e.g. CVE-2021-44228). ONE build tool, create_security_context — only when no context exists yet; capped 10/day per IP.

name: hibp
description: Have I Been Pwned (official, first-party). No-key PUBLIC tools: hibp_list_breaches, hibp_get_breach, hibp_get_latest_breach, hibp_list_data_classes, hibp_get_pwned_passwords_range (k-anonymity: only a 5-char hash prefix is sent). The account / domain / stealer-log / subscription / domain-verification tools REQUIRE your own HIBP OAuth (hibp.mcp scope) or API key and fail closed without one — and calling them transmits an email/domain to HIBP.
